edges.source: Repetitive inhaled antigen exposure and sensitization
edges.target: Exaggerated humoral and cellular lung immune response

edges.source: Exaggerated humoral and cellular lung immune response

edges.target: Fibrotic remodeling of interstitial lung architecture

edges.source: Fibrotic remodeling of interstitial lung architecture

nodes.description: Immune-driven inflammation around bronchioles produces lymphocytic infiltrates and poorly formed non-caseating granulomas.

evidence.reference_title: Hypersensitivity pneumonitis: A fibrosing alveolitis produced by inhalation of diverse antigens.
evidence.snippet: The histology of HP reveals prominent lymphocyte infiltrates that thicken the alveolar septa with poorly formed granulomas or giant cells.

nodes.description: Sensitized lungs mount exaggerated humoral and cellular immune activation involving small airways and parenchyma.
nodes.id: Exaggerated humoral and cellular lung immune response

evidence.snippet: Immunopathologically, HP is characterized by an exaggerated humoral and cellular immune response affecting the small airways and lung parenchyma.

nodes.description: Ongoing inflammation can transition to fibrotic remodeling with reticulation, traction bronchiectatic change, and honeycombing.
nodes.id: Fibrotic remodeling of interstitial lung architecture

evidence.reference_title: Hypersensitivity pneumonitis: A fibrosing alveolitis produced by inhalation of diverse antigens.
evidence.snippet: Abnormalities on high-resolution computed tomographic examinations range from nodular centrilobular opacities in acute/subacute disease to increased reticular markings and honeycombing fibrosis, which typically are predominant in the upper lobes, in patients with advanced disease.

nodes.description: Advanced fibrotic HP can follow a progressive phenotype with restrictive impairment and poor medium-term outcomes despite exposure cessation.

evidence.snippet: Some patients with fibrotic HP may evolve to a progressive phenotype, even with complete exposure avoidance.

evidence.reference_title: Long-term outcomes in chronic hypersensitivity pneumonitis.
evidence.snippet: CONCLUSION: CHP is a severe disease with a bad mid-term prognosis.

nodes.description: Inhalation of environmental antigens such as bird proteins and fungal material in susceptible individuals initiates immune sensitization and disease onset.
nodes.id: Repetitive inhaled antigen exposure and sensitization

evidence.snippet: Hypersensitivity pneumonitis (HP) is a complex syndrome caused by the inhalation of a variety of antigens in susceptible and sensitized individuals.
